key_points: MLK dorm rooms are small with shared bathrooms for 4 people
Parents report needing to deep-clean walls with Pine-Sol on move-in day
ASU reportedly assigned roommates without student permission mid-semester
key_quotes: I literally washed the walls with a Swiffer and Pine Sol in my son's dorm rooms each year. Freshman year I saw dust on the walls and that was it for me.
Can ASU or any college just give me a roommate in a week into school without my permission?
New ASU student housing includes 'lazy river' pool and virtual golf

key_points: HBCU culture at Alabama State is a genuine selling point — band, Greek life, game-day traditions are central
ASU Tempe's party school reputation is real but overstated; most students don't live that life
Transfer students and introverts both report difficulty breaking into existing social circles
key_quotes: I will literally go to a HBCU I don't wanna go no where else.
Even at the biggest party school, it's a small percentage of students that actually live the party life.
I really am not enjoying my experience and fear that it won't get any better.

key_points: A cafeteria fight at Alabama State went viral, embarrassing current students
No substantive positive dining feedback found in either source
ASU Tempe has multiple on-campus food options including Chick-fil-A and five Starbucks
key_quotes: I'm so embarrassed for every student that was involved in nonsense fight... So glad I did not attend ASU!
I am a student here, and this is not something I am proud of. The entire student body should not be judged by the actions of 40 clowns.
Ik my school isn't a t20 (ASU) but we not only have a Chick-fil-A... but also one on campus? We also have five Starbucks on campus.

key_points: Multiple students report depression, anxiety, and feeling overwhelmed shortly after starting
One student describes suicidal ideation while enrolled, citing family trauma and isolation
ASU Online's accelerated format causes burnout, especially for working students and parents
key_quotes: I can't see myself living past 30 so I am starting to wonder what I am even doing this degree.
I am a 17 year old college freshman at ASU... I feel so overwhelmed. I keep telling myself it's just freshman year jitters but I just feel like that's nonsense.
I have had it down to a science but lately its not working... I find when I get a pocket of freetime I have zero motivation.

key_points: Alabama State admitted a student with a 1410 SAT but couldn't provide enough funding to make attendance viable
ASU (Tempe) is actively reducing merit scholarships, a 'worrying and continuing pattern'
ASU Online is free for Starbucks employees, making it a genuine access option for working adults
key_quotes: My daughter has been admitted for Fall 2025. I really looked forward to it but even with 1410, we couldn't get enough funding to realize the Hornet dream.
ASU has been reducing merit scholarships and this seems to be a worrying and continuing pattern. I think decreasing state funding is affecting school budget.
I'm currently at ASU paying 26k out of my pocket. Is it worth it to try and transfer to UGA for in state tuition?

key_points: Alabama State is an HBCU with strong tradition in producing Black professionals; academics praised in community context
ASU Tempe engineering described as 'easy to get in, hard to get out' — graduates reportedly respected
ASU's accelerated online format is genuinely challenging; many students fall behind or withdraw
key_quotes: ASU engineering is actually pretty good. Their approach is that it's easy to get in but hard to get out with an engineering degree.
Only 23 of my 80 something credits are going towards my degree.
These young ppl are at University studying to get their degrees. They will be Doctors, Business people, Leaders and Trailblazers.

key_points: ASU denied one student, then auto-enrolled them in a different major without asking
Credit transfer issues are a recurring complaint — students lose dozens of credits
ASU's bureaucracy described as nightmare-level by one sophomore dealing with course registration errors
key_quotes: They did not accept me for that program but they did send a letter back stating they took the liberty of submitting and accepting me as a transfer for a BS in 'International Trade'.
I woke up to a nightmare scenario... I joined the university during the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic and I am also not that great with technology.
Only 23 of my 80 something credits are going towards my degree. I only have 12 months left of my GI Bill.

key_points: Montgomery, Alabama has high crime; Birmingham area also cited for serious safety concerns
Alabama State's campus has traditional HBCU charm — band marches through campus on game day
ASU Tempe campus is large and modern; Phoenix-level heat is a real deterrent for some
key_quotes: ASU Alum here!!! We used to march toward downtown Montgomery (straight shot from the yard). Best time of my life and I'm from Chicago!
I moved here a year ago, I wouldn't recommend it, if you want to have a life and have things to do outside of going to a college football game.
fine with heat but not phoenix level heat (so no asu)

key_points: Alabama State grads noted as producing doctors, lawyers, and business leaders — HBCU network is real
ASU Tempe has strong regional employer ties in Arizona; less recognized nationally for most majors
Pre-med students warned that ASU lacks the research opportunities of UNC or UW
key_quotes: To say that you would have the same research opportunities at ASU that you would at UNC or UW as a pre med is delusional.
It's clearly shown that more students from SJSU, UCSD, and ASU are in companies at Silicon Valley than T20s like Princeton, UPenn, Columbia.
Year after year, the HBCUs graduate a new field of educated black professionals.

key_points: ASU out-of-state at $26-28k/year is questioned by multiple students and one professor
Alabama State admitted students at 2.0 GPA with significantly lower costs than flagship schools
Free ASU Online via Starbucks partnership is a standout value for working adults
key_quotes: Parent here who went to 'elite' private school. UMich is cool, but it's not worth $50k/year. ASU definitely not worth $28k/year unless you're doing astro or maybe some other specialty.
ASU at scholarship is pending and at max they mentioned it won't cross 3k. Cost of living here was estimated around 14k. Tuition fees is 56k.
I got accepted to ASU for their ME program I was jumping for joy... Even though they're my safety school.

key_points: Alabama State has genuine pride, community, and HBCU culture that students love deeply
ASU Tempe is a sprawling commuter-ish school where you build your own experience or get lost
Both schools attract students who chose them for access or affordability, and many thrive there
key_quotes: ASU is kind of an enigma, they are top rated both nationally and internationally in a lot of areas, but they don't want anything to do with the whole selectivity nonsense.
I cannot wait to pack my backpack Sunday night, go to bed early, grab my stuff, ride the bus to campus and attend my first class as an ASU student.
Welcome to The Alabama State University!!! He will have a wonderful freshman year... the band, the sororities and fraternities, the academics, and the extracurricular activities.

key_points: **Administration:** ASU auto-enrolled students in wrong majors and loses transfer credits without warning
**Financial Aid:** Alabama State couldn't fund a 1410 SAT student; ASU cutting merit scholarships annually
**Safety:** Montgomery and Birmingham both flagged for high crime rates near campus
key_quotes: They took the liberty of submitting and accepting me as a transfer for a BS in 'International Trade' — I don't know what this degree consists of.
Overall Crime: Increased by 7.6% in the first half of 2025 compared to the first half of 2024. I think I'll pass.
Even with 1410, we couldn't get enough funding to realize the Hornet dream.

key_points: Alabama State's Mighty Marching Hornets is a ~300-member band — a genuine cultural experience
Barrett Honors College at ASU offers small classes and research perks inside a large state school
ASU's Summer Bridge program eases freshman transition for underprepared or first-gen students
key_quotes: The Mighty Marching Hornets band is roughly a 300 member organization... an essential element of HBCU culture.
Apply to Barrett. Honors colleges at decent state schools really aren't too bad.
Instead of starting in the fall, my son will be attending the Summer Bridge program in a few days for six weeks.
